hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 regions, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target (SSTR3/SSTR5) binding by avoiding unfavorable interactions in conserved pockets. This combination will increase the selectivity margin by improving SSTR2 affinity and reducing off-target affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy produces strong binders but lacks structural robustness. Tightening clash constraints and focusing mutations on non-pharmacophore positions should improve both structural quality and selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Structural failures suggest poor clash resolution in the binding pocket. Tightening the clash threshold will enforce better geometry and reduce false positives.
To improve selectivity, we need to disrupt conserved contacts with SSTR3/SSTR5 at non-pharmacophore positions while preserving pharmacophore positions 7-10 and Cys3/Cys14.
